refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to reduce effective packet loss.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control to adjust transmission parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication adaptation and teleoperation control logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality control within teleoperation data streams, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are measurable and stable enough for adaptive algorithms to be effective.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring as part of the adaptive control to mitigate responsiveness risks.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and data stream processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics such as packet loss to the NGHE Solution module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks from the NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not directly responsible for communication quality control.
Physical vehicle components have minimal role in communication packet loss management.
Primary module for teleoperation communication quality management.
Safety alerts may be influenced by communication quality but not responsible for packet loss control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality control, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential for increased complexity in Teleoperation module if communication adaptation logic grows.
Dependency on accurate network condition data from external sources.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider splitting communication adaptation into submodules if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining packet loss within limits.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential risk if network interface changes affect multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and validating against customer limits.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add latency monitoring to FR and DP to address teleoperation responsiveness.
Ensure network condition monitoring is reliable and integrated.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Gate reviews pass with manageable risks and clear ownership.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: Incorporate latency monitoring to address teleoperation responsiveness risk.

assumptions: Network conditions are measurable and stable enough for adaptive algorithms.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with selected DP.
open_risks: Latency impact of adaptive bitrate algorithms on teleoperation control.
Dependency on external network condition monitoring accuracy.
evidence_needed: Test data on packet loss and latency under representative network conditions.
Validation of adaptive algorithm effectiveness in maintaining communication quality.
